symptoms.symptom_name: Raw, weeping, superficial skin loss after a friction/shearing fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanism plus immediate raw, weeping, stinging superficial skin loss is characteristic of a partial-thickness abrasion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible embedded gravel/dirt the patient cannot remove

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impacted particulate matter defines the wound as contaminated and drives the need for decontamination and tetanus prophylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense localised stinging/burning pain aggravated by contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exposed superficial dermal nerve endings produce the disproportionate stinging typical of superficial abrasions rather than deep injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythema confined to wound margins; no spreading rash, pus, streaking or fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marginal erythema without proximal spread, purulence or systemic signs argues strongly against established infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wound depth partial-thickness — subcutaneous fat intact, no gaping edges

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact subcutaneous fat and absent wound gaping exclude a full-thickness injury needing sutures — a clinician inspection/probing finding, not confirmable over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal distal neurovascular status and full active knee range of motion

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: deep injury with tendon/joint or neurovascular involvement
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact sensation, perfusion and movement distal to the wound argue against deep structural involvement — a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Uncertain tetanus immunisation status (>10 years / unknown) with a contaminated wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soil/gravel-contaminated wound with a lapsed or uncertain immunisation history is tetanus-prone and mandates a tetanus-containing booster.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — afebrile (36.8°C), BP 135/85, HR 92 (mildly elevated with pain), SpO2 100%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile and haemodynamically stable, with only mild pain-related tachycardia — clinician-measured vitals, not obtainable in this chat as no home device was offered.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a fresh, clean, uninfected traumatic abrasion; a swab is reserved for a wound showing signs of established infection. Over-ordering here is an error.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain X-ray of the knee for radio-opaque foreign body / fracture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Consider in person only if deeper penetration, retained radio-opaque gravel not removable by irrigation, or bony tenderness is suspected on examination — not a routine step for a superficial abrasion.
